message:
Apply rustfmt normalization and promote zero-placeholder assertion to compile-time

Reformat long lines, function arguments, assert macro arms, use-import
ordering, and matches! expressions across 11 source and integration test
files to comply with the project's rustfmt configuration. No behavioral
changes in the formatting portion.

Additionally, convert the runtime assert!(ZERO_PLACEHOLDER_SCAN_FINDING_COUNT > 0)
in zero_placeholder_scan.rs to a const block assertion so the invariant is
enforced at compilation rather than only when the test runs, catching
accidental zeroing of the constant during refactors before any binary is
produced.

Files: law_mining.rs, parser_frontier_evidence.rs, parser_gap_inventory.rs,
persistent_cache_contract.rs, seqlock_reader_writer_contract.rs,
seqlock_rollout_guard.rs, ts_shipped_path_evidence.rs, zero_placeholder_scan.rs,
parser_frontier_evidence_integration.rs, stdlib_integration.rs,
ts_normalization_evidence_integration.rs, ts_shipped_path_evidence_integration.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
